page: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 8 shared vCPUs based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is network-attached with a local capacity of 1 GB, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top tier. Multi-core performance, however, falls into the average category, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations, consistently register in the average tier. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server achieves strong performance in text generation with a small 135M model, though prompt processing for larger 7B models is weak.

Given its shared CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, the vx1-g-8c-32g offers a cost-effective option for workloads that require high single-threaded burst capability but do not demand sustained, dedicated multi-core execution. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU resource model and the minimal 1 GB network-attached local storage, which necessitates external storage solutions for data-heavy applications. This server is best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and lightweight database operations.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 32.0 GB of system memory. It includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage and one public IPv4 address, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, particularly in single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. Due to its shared CPU allocation, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring high single-thread performance without continuous multi-core utilization. It is well-suited for general-purpose web serving, lightweight database operations, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for web serving and light databases.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and network storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and high single-threaded performance for cost-effective general-purpose workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
1 GB network-attached local storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core, database, and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Best suited for web serving, single-threaded tasks, and light database workloads
